differentials.name: thin atrophic endometrium prone to superficial ulceration
differentials.raw: thin atrophic endometrium prone to superficial ulceration

context: √  in 75% endometrial thickness < 4-5 mm ◊  Patient may forego endometrial biopsy! √  in 25% endometrial thickness of 6-15 mm √  hypoechoic mass with an overlying normal echogenic endometrium √  ± acoustic attenuation √  ± prolapse into endometrial cavity Rx:   can be removed at hysteroscopy if > 50% of mass projects into endometrial cavity √  indistinguishable from submucosal fibroid Optimal time of imaging: immediately after cessation of bleeding when endometrium is presumed to be thinnest Rx:     any focal / generalized thickness > 5 mm at transvaginal US requires further investigation (son
